feed.title: SAP Community - SAP HANA
feed.subtitle: SAP HANA Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Bank Integration through API
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We are in process of API based Bank Integration where the data will be sent in a JASON file using CPI. We are not clear on:

1. What config we need to do in our system?

2. What program we need to maintain under FBZP> Payment method in Country > Use Classic payment medium programs (RFFO*)

Any other thing we need to consider?

Thanks in advance

entry.title: Pool/parallel/ihm in HANA
entry.content: What is the significance of memory allocator - "Pool/parallel/ihm" in HANA?

entry.title: Why we have values in GR/IR account.
entry.content:
It is required by the financial standards, at the end of every FI period, the GR/RI accounts have to be split into two accounts

GR/IR Classification

  • Goods Received but not Invoiced} it is under liability
  • Goods Invoiced but not received} it is under Asset

In Sap the transaction posted for GR/IR Classification will go through all the open PO and check why we have balance.

The GR/IR Analysis/Maintenance transaction is run to write off the quantity remains in expense(loss) account.

entry.title: SAP HANA Service pack upgrade - High importance
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are planning to upgrade  HANA 2.0 SP 5 to 2.0 SPS 7 or higher or SPS 8. Will there be any Impact on existing BW and HANA models?

 

Thanks,

Prabhakar

entry.title: HANA HotSpots General Reportの生成依頼
entry.content:
S4HANA DIGITS Initial Shipment Stack から S4HANA DIGITS SPS01 へのアップグレード後、様々なビジネストランザクション、特に更新系アクションで処理速度が低下する事象が発生しており、原因分析のためGeneral Reportをもとに調査を行いたいと考えております。

PERSON_NAME HotSpots General Reportを生成いただく、または生成方法をご教示いただくことは可能でしょうか。

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Clear old data in tables DD03L, DDMTT_ISU, DDMTT_SAV (S4H)
entry.content:
Hi, experts

 

Can you help me with issues for clear old data from tables DD03L, DDMTT_ISU, DDMTT_SAV.

Maybe, somebody know how I can do this? Maybe archiving or some else standard instrument?

Thanks

entry.title: Issue: Data Not Displaying on CDS Endpoint Despite Presence in HANA Table
entry.content:
The Issue:

While working with SAP S/4HANA or SAP Cloud Platform, I encountered an issue where data in the HANA database table is not visible via the CDS endpoint, despite the data being present and accessible directly from the HANA table.


Here's a summary of the issue:

  • The data exists in the HANA table (Attached an image below).
  • A CDS view is defined to expose the data via an OData endpoint.
  • The OData service is properly registered, and the metadata is available.
  • However, when consuming the OData service, no data is returned, even though it should be.

 

 

Troubleshooting Steps We Tried:

Here are the troubleshooting steps we attempted to resolve the issue:

  1. Clearing Cache and Restarting Services
  2. Reviewing the CDS View Definition
  3. Verifying Service Registration
  4. Checking User Authorization

Investigating HANA CLI Query Results

Seeking Help from the SAP Community:

As I have encountered this issue in my own project, I would like to reach out to the SAP community for assistance. If anyone has experienced this problem or has insights on what might be causing this discrepancy, your input would be greatly appreciated.


I would love to hear from anyone in the SAP community who has encountered this issue before. Please share your insights, troubleshooting tips, or solutions to help resolve this issue. Your input will be invaluable in understanding and overcoming this challenge.

SAP Community

Node.js 

SAP HANA Cloud 

SAP HANA service for SAP BTP

entry.title: Calculation Scenario Filter does not work as expected
entry.content:
Running this query, we expect the calculation scenario return some rows; however, it does not.

If it runs on Hana Cloud, it works as expected, but not in Hana2.

There is a filter in the second stage from the bottom of the calc scenario like: "expression": "not in (\"VALUE\", '''') or isNull(\"VALUE\")"

If we change it we get different results:

"not in (\"VALUE\", ''1'') or isNull(\"VALUE\")" returns some rows (expected) "in (\"VALUE\", ''1'') or isNull(\"VALUE\")" returns no rows (not expected)

 
SET SCHEMA TENANT_TEST;
 
DROP TABLE "src";
CREATE COLUMN TABLE "src"(
"Plant" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Account" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202501" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202502" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202503" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202504" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202505" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202506" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202507" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202508" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202509" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202510" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202511" NVARCHAR(5000),
"Col_202512" NVARCHAR(5000)
) UNLOAD PRIORITY 5 AUTO MERGE;
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3014, 'VV311', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3012, 'VV121', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3014, 'VV300', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3012, 'VV083', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3014, 'VV093', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3012, 'VV120', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3014, 'VV122', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3012, 'VV317', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3014, 'VV082', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3012, 'EXP_FV', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3014, 'EXP_NS_PPV', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3012, 'EXP_TPC', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
INSERT INTO "src" ("Plant", "Account", "Col_202501", "Col_202502", "Col_202503", "Col_202504", "Col_202505", "Col_202506", "Col_202507", "Col_202508", "Col_202509", "Col_202510", "Col_202511", "Col_202512") 
VALUES (3012, 'EXP_BA', '', '', '', '', '', 0.6, 0.7, 0.8, 0.9, 1, 1.1, 1.2);
 
 
DROP VIEW "TENANT_TEST"."myView";
DROP CALCULATION SCENARIO "TENANT_TEST"."myCalc";
CREATE CALCULATION SCENARIO "TENANT_TEST"."myCalc" USING '[{"__CalculationNode__": true,"name": "DataSource_1","operation": {"__TableDSNodeData__": true,"source": {"__IndexName__": true,"schema": "TENANT_TEST","name": "src"},"dataSourceFlags": 0},"attributeVec": [{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Plant","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Account","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Col_202501","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Col_202502","role": 1,"datatype": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Plant","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Account","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0}]},{"__CalculationNode__": true,"name": "FILTER_4","inputVec": [{"__Input__": true,"name": "UNPIVOT_3_2"}],"operation": {"__ProjectionOpNodeData__": true},"attributeVec": [{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "KEY","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 11,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "VALUE","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 11,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Plant","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Account","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0}],"filterObject": {"__Filter__": true,"type": 0,"expression": "not in (\"VALUE\", '''') or isNull(\"VALUE\")"}},{"__CalculationNode__": true,"name": "FILTER_5","inputVec": [{"__Input__": true,"name": "FILTER_4"}],"operation": {"__ProjectionOpNodeData__": true},"attributeVec": [{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "KEY","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 11,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "VALUE","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 11,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Plant","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0},{"__Attribute__": true,"name": "Account","role": 1,"datatype": {"__DataType__": true,"type": 83,"sqlType": 37,"sqlLength": 5000},"attributeType": 0}],"filterObject": {"__Filter__": true,"type": 0,"expression": "in (\"Account\", ''VV311'', ''VV121'', ''VV300'', ''VV083'', ''VV093'', ''VV120'', ''VV122'', ''VV317'', ''VV082'', ''EXP_FV'', ''EXP_NS_PPV'', ''EXP_TPC'', ''EXP_BA'')"}}]' WITH PARAMETERS ( 'FORCE_CREATE'='1' );
 
CREATE COLUMN VIEW "TENANT_TEST"."myView" WITH PARAMETERS (indexType=11,
'PARENTCALCINDEXSCHEMA'='TENANT_TEST','PARENTCALCINDEX'='myCalc','PARENTCALCNODE'='FILTER_5');
 
select * from "myView";

entry.title: Optimizing the Code GROUP BY in loop
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can i know an alternate way of using the below code which was existing already in the system.

We observed that this statement causes the performance issues.

Kindly guide me the same. Thanks.

      LOOP AT lt_sa_from_init_screen ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<sa>)
        GROUP BY (  vkorg = <sa>-vkorg
                    vtweg = <sa>-vtweg )
        WITHOUT MEMBERS ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<sa_group>).
        INSERT VALUE mdg_ecc_bp_vkorg_vtweg_key(
          vkorg = <sa_group>-vkorg
          vtweg = <sa_group>-vtweg ) INTO TABLE lt_sales_keys.
      ENDLOOP.

 

Regards,

Karthik s

entry.title: Possible to add a new supplier whose master data doesnot exist in S4?
entry.content: Is it possible to add a supplier in RFQ which doesn't exist in S4/HANA. What about one time supplier?

entry.title: Copying condition type from Quotation to Sales Order
entry.content:
Hi,

I have a scenario where we have a custom condition type ZL03. It is a header condition and we are able to maintain the value in the quotation as expected.

But, when we are trying to create Sales order wrt quotation, this condition type ZL03 is being greyed out in the SO Header level whereas it is having the option to edit in SO item level.

This is not the expected behaviour. We need the option to edit this condition type in SO Header level and grey out in SO item level.

How can this be resolved?

entry.title: SAP B1 HANA memory issue
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We are running on SAP B1 ON HANA With 10.0 Version FP 2111. (Not B1 for Cloud)

We have 10 Users of SAP B1 (2 Prof + 8 Lim Logistics) & we use one block Hana Engine license to use SAP B1 HANA.

We face SLD issue frequently & our cloud partner informs us that HANA ran out of memory so it is the reason for SLD Service failure.

However after restart of services it resumes to function as normal & we will not face any issue for a month(approx) again same happens, Since this happens cyclic, we would like to understand what is the actual root cause.

As from cloud provider we have the root cause as HANA memory is low and need to increase memory to 2 blocks 128 Gb of memory.

We would like to calculate, estimate how much of memory is required for our database

  • We tried calculating based on three ways using below query we got 1.25 GB
with CST AS (
select SCHEMA_NAME,SUM(MEMORY_SIZE_IN_TOTAL/1024/1024/1024) as memGB
from M_CS_TABLES
group by SCHEMA_NAME)
select * from CST

order by memGB desc
  • After Backup of HANA database Uncompressed Size (1.12 GB)
  • After Backup of HANA database Compressed Size (511 MB)

Our calculation was very basic, 1.25 GB* 20 times of size = 25 GB

(10x for Size + 10x for operation capacity)

But still we have not reached 64 GB of HANA memory. Can HANA experts provide us the correct guidance pls on extending our HANA memory Block?

Thank you in advance

Best Regards

Anand Ramachandran

SAP HANA  SAP Business One SAP HANA Cloud 

@Laszlo @simon25 @Laszlo_Thoma @Cocquerel @PierreBrothier @pfefferf

entry.title: What is the significance of Operation detail verification in Order type dependent parameter - PI
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

Can you please explain the  significance of Operation detail verification in Order type dependent parameter - Process Industry as attached in the picture below.

In help it states that "All the data contained in the operation detail screens is to be checked when transferring operations from the routing into the order", but my question is :- The same happens when an Order is created with Production version, then what is it's significance?

Thank you.

entry.title: STO delivery doesn't reduce the PIR
entry.content:
Dear experts 

In SAP version 2023, we have some finished products managed with strategy 40. 

We have set the masterdata to be able to consumume the STO. Mixed MRP is 1 and the consumption indicator for planning requirements is 3. =>therefore when  STO is planned it reduces the PIRs. 

But when the delivery is created and after the post good issue the PIR reduction disappear and the WIThdrawal Qty MD73 is not updated. 

There is the note 

1819952

Stock transfer: Control the reduction of Planned Independent Requirements

but it doesn't help enough. In one hand the notes says

In the Customizing 'Assignment of Requirement types to Transaction' (Transaction OVZI) the field 'Origin of
requirement type in requirement type determination' is empty for the delivery item category and material MRP type
combination. If the value of this field is blank, the settings from the standard requirement class/type, assigned to the
planning strategy defined in the material master, will be considered.
In this case, the stock transfer will behave as a the sales order and reduce PIR

In other hand the notes says

For the STO delivery item category and MRP type combination you should Assign the value '1' to the field 'Assignment of
Requirement types to Transaction'. That way system will consider the requirement type defined here during the PIR reduction,
instead of the requirement type linked to the material master strategy.

HERE BELOW OUR SETTINGS

OVZI setting FOR THE ITEM DEL CAT

 

THANKS IN ADVANCE FOR YOUR SUPPORT 

@Caetano

entry.title: Hybrid LOB: INSERT/UPSERT Performance
entry.content:
Dear HANA Experts,

i am considering converting some VARBINARY Columns to Hybrid LOB and i am curious on the performance impact for WRITE operations. For READ it is clear to me that all LOB Data bigger some threshold (>1KB in default) is stored on Disk. So there is some I/O overhead for this.

But for the WRITE Performance, i am not sure: is there some additionally WRITE I/O for INSERT/UPSERT necessary, besides the usual sync. commit on LOG disk? is for Hybrid LOB delta store in use? 

My concern originates from SAP note 2000002, point 10: "Increased runtime of INSERT operation on table with hybrid LOB field" -> "INSERTs in hybrid LOBs have to perform disk I/O if the configured memory threshold is exceeded and the data is stored in a disk LOB."

I hope some Experts here in the HANA community can help me with this.

Thank you very much

BR Dominik

entry.title: Fetching Tax Categories from Sales org in sap mdg BP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using the MDG for BP.

We use Class CL_MDG_BS_ECC_BP_CHECK_SERVICE of method DFLT_TAX_IND_READ_BY_VKORG to fetch the tax categories based on multiple Sales org.

Noticed that this method holds multiple nested loop with multiple select queries.

Is there any alternate SAP NOTE or a different class to achieve the same.

noticed that 2795026 note is already in place.

Regards,

Karthik S

entry.title: Clarification on reverse implementation of S4HANA from R4 to R2
entry.content:
Dear Experts, kindly guide on a customer merger situation wherein both are on SAP HANA. However, due to merger they need to bring one of the version down from R4 to R2 so that they have one consistent platform. Please advise how to move forward?

Please advise if you have encountered similar situation, and can share the approach /guidelines/tools/techniques to handle the customer merger situation.

Thanks in advance, look forward to hearing back.

entry.title: Is it possible to reset Cumulative Received Quantities in Schedule Agreement Items every X time?
entry.content:
Dear experts,

I am currently analyzing a requirement with my client in order to reset Cumulative Quantities received for a specific item in a Schedule Agreement from Vendors. 

So far, I am not able to find any "**bleep**. GR Qty" anywhere in the Schedule Lines but only the "**bleep**. Sch. Qty" and the "GR Qty" specific for one delivery schedule. 

1. Is there any field available to identify the cumulative received quantity? If not, I understand we would need to calculate this based on the GR Qtities for an item, right?

2. If a valid field can be used for this, is it possible to reset it every period of time as we would do for the Agreed Cumulative Qty or is that something that can only be done for Agree Qtities?

Based on your knowlegde, I might probably need to start using the field "Agreed Cumulative Quantity" so this should be defined before it is reset as of 0 for the next period of time. Since I have never worked with this, I have an additional question:

3. By using ME88, are we only resetting this field or are we resetting actually the Cumulative Qty?

 

Thanks a lot for your inputs!!!

entry.title: Purchase order Release strategy's status is wrong in ME23N
entry.content:
The purchase order workflow is completed, and all the approvers are approved. The PO has been released but is still in ME23N, and the status of the Release strategy is pending in the header details. 
In the workflow log, there is no error, and all the tasks are executed perfectly, what is the root cause for this behavior in the s4 Hana system, and how to resolve this?

entry.title: Goods issue in S/4 without using EWM for delivery note printout
entry.content:
Hi All,

We are using S/4 2022. I just want to confirm.

Delivery note cannot be printed without doing a goods issue in outbound delivery? is my assumption correct?
